title: Making Choices (if/else) рҹӣЈпёҸ
description: Teach your code to make decisions just like a human.

slides.title: The Fork in the Road рҹӣЈпёҸ
slides.content:
# The `if` Statement

Up until now, our code ran from top to bottom, executing every single line. But what if we only want to run code *sometimes*?

We use an `if` statement! It checks a condition. If the condition is true, it runs the code inside its curly braces `{ }`.

```swift
let isRaining = true

if isRaining {
    print("Bring an umbrella! вҳ”пёҸ")
}
```

quizData.question: If you set 'isRaining = false' in the previous code, what happens?

options.text: Nothing prints. The block is skipped.

quizData.explanation: If the condition is false, Swift completely skips over the code inside the curly braces `{ }`.

slides.title: Plan B: Introduction to 'else' рҹӣЎпёҸ

quizData.question: What is the purpose of an 'else' block?

options.text: To provide code that runs if the initial condition is FALSE.
To stop the program from running.
To run the exact same code twice.

quizData.explanation: An `else` statement is the fallback or 'Plan B' that executes exclusively when the `if` condition evaluates to false.

slides.title: See It in Action
slides.content: Try changing `isHungry` to `false` and run the code again to see how the logic paths change!
